zotero.abstractNote: State Space Models (SSMs) like Mamba have recently emerged as a promising alternative to Transformers for sequence modeling. While their performance on large-scale pre-training is well-documented, their behavior in low-data regimes remains under-explored. In this work, we investigate the low-shot transferability of Vision Mamba ([V]-Mamba) models. We find that [V]-Mamba exhibits competitive performance compared to Vision Transformers (ViT) in low-shot settings, while offering computational advantages. We provide empirical evidence across multiple datasets and analyze the factors contributing to this transferability.
